target: 7. Light and Shadow
Alright, we're heeeere! This is the last stop!
The final stop is Koba Mart, huh... I'm quite familiar with this place as well.
It's not open for business, so is today their regular day off? There shouldn't be anything we can do here on their day off.
There is, there is! This is my friend's house.
What a coincidence, this is a friend's house for me as well.
Huh? Are you friends with Shiori-chan too, Hizuru-chan?
No, that's not it. My friend is...
…Hizuru?

Ah, Asako.
It's been a while.
Wh-When did you get back!? Why didn't you contact me!
Sorry. I didn't have the time to--
No, actually, I'm currently in a situation where I don't have the time for that.
Hello~!

Hello, Hoine-chan!
You came with Hizuru?
Yeah! I'm guiding Hizuru-chan around the island.
Huh, around the island?
There's no deep meaning behind it... Ryunosuke forced me to take care of my niece.
Ahaha, so that's what it is.
Is Shiori-chan here~?
Yeah, she's inside, so go ahead and play with her! Hizuru, you come inside too!
Alright, just for a bit then.
Sorry we don't have much to offer. Here, some tea.
Ah, thanks.
So, what's up?
Huh? What is it, so suddenly.
You don't seem very energetic, though.
...I'm just tired from dealing with a child when I'm not used to it.
Even if you say a child, you're dealing with Hoine-chan, right? If it's that girl, I don't think she'd be that much of a handful.
(If it were another child, it would be even worse than this? Unbelievable...)
Hey, Hizuru…
You're not really just tired, are you? Come on, tell me honestly.
Don't underestimate your friends, okay? Even if we haven't seen each other in a while, I can tell that much.
But I won't force you to tell me. If you ever want to talk about it, just let me know anytime.
Like I said... I'm just tired.
Hehe... Yeah, yeah! Your stubbornness hasn't changed a bit!
Ahaha! Wait, wait~~!
Hehe~n! You'll never catch me~~!
Hey! Don't run around! You're gonna fall!
Okaaaay!
Jeez, her replies are the only good thing about her.
Is that child your daughter, Asako?
Yeah, Shiori. Sorry, my kid's so loud.
It's not a problem. You were quite something in the past too, Asako.
B-By the past, you mean when we were students!? Was I really that loud!?
No, it seems you're still going strong.
Ahahahahahaha! You got me there!
But once you have a kid, any parent becomes like this, you know?
Is that how it is?
Yeah, yeah, that's how it is. Don't you want kids, Hizuru?
I don't even have a partner in the first place. Parthenogenesis is impossible for humans.
Parthenogenesis?
Right now, I have neither the interest nor the imagination to create my own family.
But as a theme for a novel, it's a universal subject. I have an interest in it in that sense, though.
(Family... Household... Parents... Children... Parent and child...)
(Family, parent and child... It might be a hint for something, but my knowledge alone isn't enough information.)
Asako, what is a child?
There it is! A sudden question!
Back when you were on the island, Hizuru, I used to get hit with sudden questions like that all the time and it was really troublesome.
Was that so?
Intellectual curiosity? I guess you just have a lot of that.
Really, you're exactly the same as you used to be, Hizuru. I'm relieved to see you're still going strong.
Enough with the incomprehensible admiration, just answer my question from before.
Hmm, let's see... To me, a child is...
…Light, I guess.
Light…?
Yeah, light. A light that brightly illuminates everything.
Just having Shiori around makes every day brighter.
The atmosphere in the house, the feelings and expressions of the whole family, she brightens everything up.
Hmm...
Then if the child is the light, what are you parents?
If the child is the light, then the parent is the shadow... I guess? You know, shadows always accompany light.
A shadow that stays close, a shadow that watches from afar, I think parents in this world are the ones who continue to support their children in various forms.
But you know, if a child ever loses their light, then the parent becomes the light and illuminates the child.
By influencing each other like that... I think that's how parents and children grow together.
I see... Light and shadow are two sides of the same coin, and their forms are not singular, huh.
Was that helpful at all?
Hizuru?
(Light and shadow... child and parent...)
(The child who was the light will also one day become a parent and a shadow...)
Oh boy, she's entered her own world.
(If you change the way it's illuminated even slightly, the light will create shadows of various sizes and shapes...)
(Light... Shadow... Which one am I right now, anyway?)
Hey, Hizuru! Hi~zu~ru!!
Hizuru-chan, it's time to go!
Are you okay? Haven't you gotten more complicated than before?
Don't worry about it...
Thanks for having us!
Bye-bye~!
Come again, you two.
Okaaaay!
*Sigh*...
So your friend was Shiori-chan's mom, Hizuru-chan.
That's right...
(Child and parent... Light and shadow... I got some words that might be a hint, but...)
(It still seems like I'm far from breaking out of this slump...)
